worth_it_explanation: Matched Audiences are where the warm prospects live. Site visitors, named contacts, and target company lists are the people most likely to convert, and reaching them takes a few audiences that the account can build once and reuse. The checks are quick to run because everything lives on the Audiences page in Campaign Manager, where the audience type, size, and match status are visible without leaving the platform. The payoff is concrete. An account that retargets and runs account-based lists spends on people it has already identified rather than paying full prospecting prices to find them again.
intro: Matched Audiences let an account reach people it has already identified rather than relying on broad targeting alone. They include website retargeting built from Insight Tag data, uploaded contact lists, and company lists for account-based marketing. These checks confirm that the account is actually using these audiences, that retargeting pools are segmented by how deeply a member engaged, and that uploaded lists match well and rest on a lawful basis.

audit_checks.title: Matched Audiences are not used for retargeting or account-based marketing
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without Matched Audiences, the account reaches warm site visitors and named target accounts only by chance through broad attribute targeting. Spend goes to finding prospects from scratch when many have already been identified, and account-based marketing has no audience to run against.

educate.base:
Matched Audiences are audience segments built from data the account already has, rather than from LinkedIn's attribute targeting alone. Website retargeting uses the LinkedIn Insight Tag to build an audience of members who visited specific pages. Contact lists target named people by their professional or personal email. Company lists target named organizations, which is the core mechanism for account-based marketing (ABM) on LinkedIn.

When none of these are in use, the account reaches people only through job title, function, seniority, industry, and similar facets. Those facets find prospects who match a profile, but they do not single out the members who already visited the site or the accounts a sales team has named as targets. Those warm contacts then get reached by chance, mixed in with everyone else who fits the broad profile, at full prospecting cost.

A few signs point to Matched Audiences sitting unused:
  • The Audiences page shows no website, contact list, or company list audiences, or shows them built but never attached to a campaign
  • Every active campaign targets by attributes alone, with no retargeting layer
  • A sales or ABM target-account list exists in the CRM but was never uploaded to Campaign Manager
You may want to confirm which Matched Audiences exist, which are actually attached to live campaigns, and whether a target-account list is available to upload. An audience that is built but unattached does no work.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a considered-purchase or business-to-business (B2B) store, retargeting visitors who reached a product or pricing page keeps the brand in front of buyers through a long evaluation. You may want to confirm the Insight Tag fires on those pages so a retargeting audience can form.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-capture site, Matched Audiences carry most of the weight. Retargeting form-page visitors who did not submit, and uploading a target-account list for ABM, are usually the highest-value audiences. You may want to confirm both exist and are attached to live campaigns.

learn_more.source_title: Get started with LinkedIn Matched Audiences
Upload company and contact targeting lists

investigate.intro: Confirm in Campaign Manager which Matched Audiences exist, whether they are attached to live campaigns, and whether a target-account list is available to upload.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: In Campaign Manager, open 'Plan', then 'Audiences', to see every Matched Audience the account has built and its current member count and status.
Note whether any website, contact list, or company list audiences exist, and whether each is large enough to use, which on LinkedIn means at least 300 member accounts.
Open the active campaigns and, in each campaign's audience settings, check whether a Matched Audience is attached or whether the targeting is attribute-based alone.
Ask whether a sales or ABM target-account list exists in the CRM. If one does and no matching company-list audience appears on the Audiences page, the list has not been put to work.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records which Matched Audience types exist (website, contact list, company list), whether each is attached to at least one live campaign, and whether a target-account list exists but was never uploaded. An account with relevant audiences built and attached passes. An account with no Matched Audiences, with audiences built but unattached, or with a known target-account list that was never uploaded, is a fail recorded with the gap.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record which Matched Audiences exist, which are attached to live campaigns, and whether a target-account list is available to upload, to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} is not using Matched Audiences to retarget site visitors or to run account-based campaigns against named target accounts. The account reaches prospects through attribute targeting alone, so warm visitors and target accounts are reached only by chance and at full prospecting cost. {{voice_recommend}} building a website retargeting audience from the Insight Tag, uploading the target-account list as a company-list audience for account-based marketing, and attaching these audiences to live campaigns. After the change, confirm on the Audiences page that each audience has cleared the 300-member minimum and is in use.

audit_checks.title: Retargeting audiences are not segmented by engagement depth
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A single undifferentiated retargeting pool sends the same message to a member who only saw the homepage and to one who opened a Lead Gen Form. The deeper-intent members get a generic ad instead of the next-step message that fits their stage, and budget spreads evenly across very different levels of interest.

educate.base:
Retargeting on LinkedIn can be built from more than a single 'all site visitors' pool. The Insight Tag supports website audiences scoped to specific page URLs, and Campaign Manager also offers engagement-based audiences such as members who viewed a share of a video, opened or submitted a Lead Gen Form, or interacted with a single image ad. Each of these marks a different depth of interest.

A member who opened a Lead Gen Form or reached a pricing page is closer to acting than one who landed on the homepage and left. When a single retargeting audience holds all of them together, every member sees the same creative, so the higher-intent members get a generic reminder rather than the next-step message their stage calls for. Budget then spreads evenly across people whose interest is anything but even.

Segmenting retargeting means building separate audiences along the engagement path, for example:
  • Pricing-page or product-page visitors, scoped by URL
  • Lead Gen Form openers who did not submit
  • Video viewers who watched most of a clip
You may want to confirm whether retargeting is a single pool or a set of stage-specific audiences, and whether the creative attached to each one matches that stage. A segmented setup lets the message advance as the member's interest does.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a considered-purchase or business-to-business (B2B) store, separating product or pricing-page visitors from general browsers lets the closer-to-buying group see an offer or proof point rather than a brand reminder. You may want to confirm the Insight Tag can scope an audience to those URLs.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-capture site, the highest-value retargeting segment is usually members who opened a Lead Gen Form but did not submit. You may want to confirm that audience exists on its own and carries a message built to finish the form.

investigate.intro: Confirm in Campaign Manager whether retargeting is a single visitor pool or a set of audiences segmented by engagement depth, and whether each carries stage-appropriate creative.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: In Campaign Manager, open 'Plan', then 'Audiences', and filter to website and engagement-based audiences.
Note whether retargeting is one broad 'all visitors' audience or several audiences scoped to specific pages, form interactions, or video views.
Open the campaigns that use these audiences and check whether the creative attached to a deeper-intent audience differs from the creative shown to general visitors.
Where a single pool covers every visitor, note that members at very different stages are receiving the same message.

browser_dev_tools.label: Browser developer tools
steps: Open the site's key pages, for example the pricing and form pages, and use the browser's network panel to confirm the LinkedIn Insight Tag fires on each.
If the tag is missing on a page, a URL-scoped retargeting audience cannot be built from it, which limits how far retargeting can be segmented.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether retargeting is a single visitor pool or a set of audiences segmented by engagement depth, and whether each segment's creative matches its stage. An account with stage-specific retargeting audiences and matching creative passes. An account with a single undifferentiated pool, or with segments that all share one generic creative, is a fail recorded with the affected audiences.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether retargeting is one pool or several stage-specific audiences, and whether each carries stage-appropriate creative, to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} retargets through a single visitor pool rather than audiences segmented by how deeply a member engaged. A homepage visitor and a member who opened a Lead Gen Form sit in the same audience and see the same ad, so the higher-intent members get a generic reminder instead of a next-step message. {{voice_recommend}} splitting retargeting into stage-specific audiences, for example pricing-page visitors, form openers who did not submit, and video viewers, and attaching creative that matches each stage. After the change, confirm on the Audiences page that the segments are populated and that each campaign serves the message built for its segment.

audit_checks.title: Uploaded lists have low match rates or are stale
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An uploaded list reaches only the contacts LinkedIn can associate with a member, so a poorly formatted or aged list matches few and the audience falls short of the 300-member minimum or covers a fraction of the intended targets. A stale list also leaves open whether the uploaded contact data still rests on a lawful basis.

educate.base:
When a contact or company list is uploaded to Campaign Manager, LinkedIn matches the rows against member accounts and reports a match rate. Only matched members can be targeted, so the match rate sets the real reach of the audience. LinkedIn requires at least 300 matched member accounts before an audience can be used, so a list that matches poorly may not run at all.

Match rate depends heavily on how the list is prepared. LinkedIn provides a template, and following it raises the match. For company lists, including the LinkedIn Company Page URL alongside the company name improves matching accuracy. For contact lists, business email matches better than personal email for a professional audience. A list exported in the wrong shape, with missing fields, or full of long-departed contacts, matches few of its rows.

Staleness adds a second concern. A list assembled years ago carries contacts who may have changed jobs, and it also raises a question about the lawful basis for using that contact data now. LinkedIn limits Matched Audience sizes in the European Economic Area (EEA) and Switzerland based on member consent, so an old list drawn from those regions may match even fewer members than its age alone would suggest.

You may want to confirm the match rate of each uploaded list, when it was last refreshed, whether it follows LinkedIn's template, and whether there is a documented lawful basis for the contact data. A list that fails any of these is reaching fewer of the right people than it appears to.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a considered-purchase or business-to-business (B2B) store, an uploaded customer or prospect list is most useful when it carries the business email and, for company targeting, the Company Page URL. You may want to confirm the export follows LinkedIn's template so the match rate holds up.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-capture site, contact lists are often the core ABM input, so a low match rate or a stale list directly shrinks the account-based audience. You may want to confirm the list is recent, formatted to template, and backed by a lawful basis for the upload.

learn_more.source_title: Upload company and contact targeting lists

investigate.intro: Confirm in Campaign Manager the match rate, recency, and formatting of each uploaded list, and whether a lawful basis for the contact data is documented.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: In Campaign Manager, open 'Plan', then 'Audiences', and select each list-based audience to see its matched size and status.
Note whether the matched size clears the 300-member minimum and how it compares with the number of rows that were uploaded, which gives a rough match rate.
Check the audience's creation or last-update date to gauge how recent the underlying list is.

Confirm with the account owner whether the upload followed LinkedIn's company or contact template and whether a lawful basis for the contact data is documented, especially for contacts in the European Economic Area or Switzerland.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per uploaded list, the matched size against the rows uploaded, the recency of the list, whether it followed LinkedIn's template, and whether a lawful basis is documented. A list with a healthy match clearing the 300-member minimum, a recent refresh, template formatting, and a documented basis passes. A list with a low match, a stale date, off-template formatting, or no documented basis is a fail recorded with the list name and the issue.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the matched size, recency, formatting, and lawful-basis status for each uploaded list to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more uploaded lists on {{site_name}} match poorly or have not been refreshed. Because only matched members can be targeted, a list that matches few of its rows reaches a fraction of the intended audience, and a list that may fall below the 300-member minimum cannot run at all. A stale list also leaves the lawful basis for the contact data unconfirmed. {{voice_recommend}} re-exporting the lists to LinkedIn's company and contact templates, including the Company Page URL for company rows and business email for contacts, refreshing aged lists, and confirming a documented lawful basis for the upload. After the change, recheck each audience's matched size on the Audiences page.
